annotations.label: Norm (informal)

anchor.snippet: a norm is a function from a real or complex vector space to the non-negative real numbers

annotations.note: Mathlib's `Norm` typeclass attaches a norm function `‖·‖` to a type, generalizing the real/complex vector-space case.

annotations.label: Seminorm (informal)

anchor.snippet: A seminorm satisfies the first two properties of a norm but may be zero for vectors other than the origin.

annotations.note: `Seminorm 𝕜 E` bundles a function satisfying absolute homogeneity and subadditivity without requiring positive-definiteness.

annotations.label: Normed vector space

anchor.snippet: A vector space with a specified norm is called a normed vector space

annotations.note: `NormedSpace 𝕜 E` extends a `NormedAddCommGroup` with a compatible scalar action, matching the Wikipedia definition.

annotations.label: Seminormed vector space

anchor.snippet: a vector space with a seminorm is called a seminormed vector space

annotations.note: Combined with `NormedSpace 𝕜 E` over a seminormed group, this furnishes the seminormed-vector-space structure.

annotations.label: Pseudonorm

anchor.snippet: The term pseudonorm has been used for several related meanings.

annotations.note: Mathlib has no declaration named `Pseudonorm` and the ambiguous informal meanings collapse into `Seminorm`/`AddGroupSeminorm` rather than a separate notion.

annotations.label: Norm (formal)

anchor.snippet: a norm on

annotations.note: `NormedAddCommGroup` packages positive-definiteness, absolute homogeneity (via the integer/scalar action), and the triangle inequality.

annotations.label: Seminorm (formal)

anchor.snippet: A seminorm on

annotations.note: `Seminorm 𝕜 E` is the bundled seminorm exactly as described.

annotations.label: Non-negativity of seminorms

anchor.snippet: Properties (1.) and (2.) imply that if

annotations.note: `norm_nonneg` proves `0 ≤ ‖x‖` from the seminorm axioms (also available as `Seminorm.nonneg` via its `convexOn`/structure API).

annotations.label: Norm notation

anchor.snippet: the norm of a vector

annotations.note: Mathlib provides the `‖·‖` notation through the `Norm` typeclass field `Norm.norm`.

annotations.label: Every vector space admits a norm

anchor.snippet: Every (real or complex) vector space admits a norm

annotations.note: Mathlib can transport a norm across a linear equivalence (e.g. with a basis), but the abstract `Every vector space admits a norm` existence theorem itself is not stated.

annotations.label: Absolute-value norm

anchor.snippet: The absolute value

annotations.note: `Real.norm_eq_abs` identifies the norm on `ℝ` with the absolute value, formalizing the one-dimensional absolute-value norm.

annotations.label: Norms on 1D space equivalent to absolute value

anchor.snippet: Any norm

annotations.note: Subsumed by the general finite-dimensional norm-equivalence theorem, but not stated as a dedicated 1-D corollary.

annotations.label: Euclidean norm

anchor.snippet: the intuitive notion of length of the vector

annotations.note: `EuclideanSpace.norm_eq` gives `‖x‖ = sqrt (∑ i, ‖x i‖^2)`, formalizing the Euclidean norm on `ℝⁿ`.

annotations.label: Coordinate-free Euclidean norm

anchor.snippet: the Euclidean norm can be written in a coordinate-free way

annotations.note: `inner_self_eq_norm_mul_norm` expresses `⟨x,x⟩ = ‖x‖ * ‖x‖`, the coordinate-free Euclidean formula.

annotations.label: Euclidean norm of complex numbers

anchor.snippet: The Euclidean norm of a complex number is the absolute value

annotations.note: Mathlib's norm on `ℂ` is the standard modulus and `Complex.isAbsoluteValueNorm` records that it is an absolute value.

annotations.label: Four Euclidean Hurwitz algebras

anchor.snippet: There are exactly four Euclidean Hurwitz algebras over the real numbers

annotations.note: Mathlib has `Quaternion` and an `Octonion`-related WIP but no formalization of Hurwitz's classification theorem.

annotations.label: Canonical norm on quaternions

anchor.snippet: The canonical norm on

annotations.note: `Quaternion.normSq` plus the `NormedRing` instance (see `Quaternion.normSq_eq_norm_mul_self` in `Mathlib.Analysis.Quaternion`) furnish the canonical quaternion norm.

annotations.label: Norm on finite-dimensional complex space

anchor.snippet: the most common norm is

annotations.note: Specialising `EuclideanSpace 𝕜 (Fin n)` to `𝕜 = ℂ` gives the standard Hermitian norm on `ℂⁿ`.

annotations.label: Norm as square root of inner product

anchor.snippet: the norm can be expressed as the square root of the inner product

annotations.note: `inner_self_eq_norm_sq` (and `inner_self_eq_norm_mul_norm`) say `⟨x,x⟩ = ‖x‖²`, so `‖x‖ = sqrt ⟨x,x⟩`.

annotations.label: Taxicab / Manhattan norm

anchor.snippet: The name relates to the distance a taxi has to drive

annotations.note: `PiLp.norm_eq_of_L1` gives `‖x‖ = ∑ i, ‖x i‖`, the L¹/taxicab norm on finite products.

annotations.label: p-norm

anchor.snippet: Let

annotations.note: `PiLp.norm_eq_sum` gives `‖x‖ = (∑ ‖x i‖^p)^(1/p)` for `0 < p`, the finite-dimensional p-norm.

annotations.label: p=2 induced by inner product

anchor.snippet: the [MATH] -norm is even induced by a canonical inner product

annotations.note: `EuclideanSpace` is `PiLp 2` with the inner-product structure; `EuclideanSpace.norm_eq` shows the resulting norm equals the L² norm.

annotations.label: p<1 not a norm

anchor.snippet: the resulting function does not define a norm

annotations.note: Mathlib defines `PiLp p` for all `p` but does not state that the p<1 expression fails the triangle inequality.

annotations.label: Partial derivative of p-norm

anchor.snippet: The partial derivative of the

annotations.note: Searches for explicit partial-derivative formulas of the p-norm in `PiLp`/`MeanInequalities` returned no relevant lemma.

annotations.label: Maximum / infinity norm

anchor.section: Maximum norm (special case of: infinity norm, uniform norm, or supremum norm)
anchor.snippet: If

annotations.note: `PiLp.norm_eq_ciSup` gives `‖f‖ = ⨆ i, ‖f i‖` on `PiLp ⊤`, formalizing the maximum/sup norm.

annotations.label: Energy norm

anchor.snippet: The energy norm

annotations.note: Mathlib has inner products but no dedicated `EnergyNorm` construction `‖x‖_A = √⟨Ax,x⟩` for a positive operator.

annotations.label: Energy norm bounds via spectrum

anchor.snippet: the value of the norm is dependent on the spectrum

annotations.note: No formalization of energy-norm spectral bounds was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Zero norm / F-norm

anchor.snippet: the zero norm induces a complete metric topology

annotations.note: Mathlib does not define an F-norm class capturing the discrete-metric `zero norm`.

annotations.label: Hamming distance from zero

anchor.section: Hamming distance of a vector from zero
anchor.snippet: the discrete distance defines the Hamming distance

annotations.note: `PiLp.norm_eq_card` says the `PiLp 0` 'norm' counts the nonzero coordinates, matching Hamming distance from zero, though `Hamming` is not directly cross-referenced.

annotations.label: Zero norm (Donoho)

anchor.section: Hamming distance of a vector from zero
anchor.snippet: the zero "norm" of

annotations.note: Same `PiLp 0` formula as Hamming distance from zero; Mathlib offers it as the `PiLp 0` norm rather than under Donoho's name.

annotations.label: Infinite-dimensional p-norms

anchor.snippet: The generalization of the above norms to an infinite number of components

annotations.note: `lp E p` (with its `Norm`) formalizes the ℓᵖ spaces of infinite sequences with finite p-norm.

annotations.label: Inner product induces a norm

anchor.snippet: Any inner product induces in a natural way the norm

annotations.note: Mathlib upgrades an `InnerProductSpace.Core` to a `NormedAddCommGroup`, exhibiting the induced norm `‖x‖ = √⟨x,x⟩`.

annotations.label: Composite norm example

anchor.snippet: Other norms on

annotations.note: The specific composite-norm examples on ℝⁿ from the article are not stated as named lemmas in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Norm pulled back by linear map

anchor.snippet: For any norm and any injective linear transformation

annotations.note: `Seminorm.comp` pulls back a seminorm along a linear map; `Equiv.normedAddCommGroup` transports a norm across an equivalence — together covering the construction without a single dedicated 'pullback norm' decl.

annotations.label: Minkowski functional norm

anchor.snippet: the Minkowski functional of a centrally-symmetric convex body

annotations.note: `gaugeSeminorm` packages the Minkowski functional of an absorbing, balanced, convex set as a `Seminorm`.

annotations.label: Galois-theoretic norm

anchor.snippet: the Galois-theoretic norm of an element

annotations.note: `Algebra.norm` is the determinant-of-left-multiplication norm form for algebra extensions, the algebraic norm referenced in the article.

annotations.label: Composition algebra

anchor.snippet: A composition algebra

annotations.note: No `CompositionAlgebra` class exists in Mathlib (`decl_exists` reports false).

annotations.label: Composition algebra homomorphism property

anchor.snippet: The characteristic feature of composition algebras is the homomorphism property

annotations.note: Without `CompositionAlgebra` Mathlib has no statement of `N(xy) = N(x)N(y)` in that generality.

annotations.label: Reverse triangle inequality

anchor.snippet: the reverse triangle inequality holds

annotations.note: `abs_norm_sub_norm_le` proves `|‖a‖ - ‖b‖| ≤ ‖a - b‖` (companion `norm_sub_norm_le` for the one-sided form).

annotations.label: Norm equals norm of transpose

anchor.snippet: the norm of

annotations.note: Operator norm equals norm of adjoint is in Mathlib for Hilbert-space adjoints, but the article's matrix-transpose statement of `‖A‖ = ‖Aᵀ‖` for the spectral norm is not isolated as a single decl.

annotations.label: Hölder's inequality

anchor.snippet: we have Hölder's inequality

annotations.note: `NNReal.inner_le_Lp_mul_Lq` (with ENNReal/MeasureTheory variants like `MeasureTheory.MemLp.smul`) formalizes Hölder for finite sums and Lᵖ functions.

annotations.label: Cauchy–Schwarz inequality

anchor.snippet: A special case of this is the Cauchy–Schwarz inequality

annotations.note: `norm_inner_le_norm` is the Cauchy–Schwarz inequality `‖⟨x,y⟩‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖`.

annotations.label: Every norm is a convex function

anchor.snippet: every norm is a convex function

annotations.note: `convexOn_norm` (and `Seminorm.convexOn`) state that the norm function is convex.

annotations.label: Convergence in norm

anchor.snippet: A sequence of vectors

annotations.note: `tendsto_iff_norm_sub_tendsto_zero` characterizes convergence in a normed group via `‖xₙ - x‖ → 0`.

annotations.label: Equivalent norms

anchor.snippet: are called equivalent if they induce the same topology

annotations.note: Mathlib speaks of two norm structures inducing the same topology / uniform structure but lacks a dedicated `EquivNorm` predicate; the notion appears informally inside finite-dimensional lemmas.

annotations.label: Finite-dimensional norms all equivalent

anchor.snippet: If the vector space is a finite-dimensional real or complex one, all norms are equivalent.

annotations.note: Mathlib proves every finite-dimensional `𝕜`-normed space is complete and any two finite-dim norm topologies coincide (via `LinearMap.continuous_of_finiteDimensional` and `ContinuousLinearEquiv.ofFinrankEq`).

annotations.label: Gauge of absolutely convex absorbing set

anchor.section: Classification of seminorms: absolutely convex absorbing sets
anchor.snippet: To each such subset corresponds a seminorm

annotations.note: `gauge` and `gaugeSeminorm` formalize the Minkowski-functional/seminorm of a balanced convex absorbing set.

annotations.label: Local basis of absolutely convex sets

anchor.section: Classification of seminorms: absolutely convex absorbing sets
anchor.snippet: Any locally convex topological vector space has a local basis consisting of absolutely convex sets.

annotations.note: Mathlib gives a convex neighborhood basis at every point, with related `absConvexHull`/`balanced` API, but no single decl asserting an *absolutely* convex local basis.

annotations.label: Kolmogorov normability criterion

anchor.section: Classification of seminorms: absolutely convex absorbing sets
anchor.snippet: any locally convex and locally bounded topological vector space is normable

annotations.note: Mathlib has `PolynormableSpace` and locally-convex/bornology infrastructure but no statement of Kolmogorov's normability theorem.
